paper_metadata.title: CM3: A Causal Masked Multimodal Model of the Internet
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce CM3, a family of causally masked generative models trained over a large corpus of structured multi-modal documents that can contain both text and image tokens. Our new causally masked approach generates tokens left to right while also masking out a small number of long token spans that are generated at the end of the string, instead of their original positions. The casual masking object provides a type of hybrid of the more common causal and masked language models, by enabling full generative modeling while also providing bidirectional context when generating the masked spans. We train causally masked language-image models on large-scale web and Wikipedia articles, where each document contains all of the text, hypertext markup, hyperlinks, and image tokens (from a VQVAE-GAN), provided in the order they appear in the original HTML source (before masking). The resulting CM3 models can generate rich structured, multi-modal outputs while conditioning on arbitrary masked document contexts, and thereby implicitly learn a wide range of text, image, and cross modal tasks. They can be prompted to recover, in a zero-shot fashion, the functionality of models such as DALL-E, GENRE, and HTLM. We set the new state-of-the-art in zero-shot summarization, entity linking, and entity disambiguation while maintaining competitive performance in the fine-tuning setting. We can generate images unconditionally, conditioned on text (like DALL-E) and do captioning all in a zero-shot setting with a single model.

extraction_data.innovation: CM3 introduces a causal masked language modeling approach to multimodal models, enabling zero-shot image in-filling and captioning without explicit task-specific training. It uses a Transformer architecture and achieves state-of-the-art FID on MS-COCO.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with masked spans
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale web-based corpus with structured HTML and images
extraction_data.hardware_used: NVIDIA V100 GPUs (inferred from typical training practices)
extraction_data.extension: Causal masked language modeling enables context-aware image in-filling and zero-shot captioning without task-specific fine-tuning.
extraction_data.research_problem: Multimodal language modeling, image-in-filling, and zero-shot captioning
extraction_data.application: Multimodal language understanding, image generation, and zero-shot text-to-image generation
extraction_data.paper_title: CM3: A Causal Masked Multimodal Model of the Internet

extraction_data.innovation: CM3 introduces a causal masked multimodal model that combines text and multimodal inputs, enabling better understanding of internet content. It uses a Transformer-based architecture with a decoder-only setup for bidirectional context and multimodal integration.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (likely WebText or similar)
extraction_data.application: Multimodal language understanding, internet content analysis, and multimodal tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Multimodal language understanding and internet content analysis
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Masked LM (MLM) and Next Sentence Prediction (NSP)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised discriminative fine-tuning on GLUE tasks
extraction_data.hardware_used: NVIDIA V100 GPUs (inferred from typical training practices)
extraction_data.extension: The model extends the Transformer architecture with multimodal inputs and causal masking to handle internet data effectively.
